title: The Light Goes Up Into Galilee
subtitle: Jesus withdraws into Galilee and begins to preach: "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand."

stillness: Be still, for the dawn goes before the sun.
John was the voice; the Word now begins to speak.
Let the night and the fog of your unfaith withdraw a little, and grow used to the light.
gospel: When Jesus had heard that John was delivered up,
he withdrew, and went again into Galilee.
And leaving the city of Nazareth, where he had been conceived and nourished,
he came and dwelt in Capharnaum, the city by the sea,
in the borders of Zabulon and of Nephthalim.
From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say:
The time is fulfilled — do penance, and believe the Gospel,
for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
behold: See why Christ fled from Herod and withdrew into Galilee.
When he heard that John was delivered up — and without doubt delivered up by God, for against one holy man no one can do anything unless God deliver him —
and when he knew that the Pharisees had heard he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John, and from this took up a greater envy against him,
he left Judea, where the unbelieving Pharisees were, envying the increase of his Baptism and his teaching, and already plotting to persecute him.
He withdrew: to give an example of patience and meekness, yielding to the evil;
to soften their envy by his absence, until they should become better-minded;
to set before us a pattern of fleeing persecutors and danger;
to preach the Gospel to others; for the time of his Passion was not yet come.
And leaving Nazareth, where he had been conceived and nourished, he came to preach in Galilee,
and dwelt in Capharnaum, by the sea, which was greater and more populous — that by preaching and working miracles he might enlighten the more.
And note: Capharnaum is said to lie between the borders of Judea and of the Gentiles.
There the corner-stone had to be founded, which made the two one, joining in himself the two walls, of the Gentiles and of the Jews.
For in Galilee both Gentiles and Jews dwelt together,
and so it was shown that he had come for the redemption of the Gentiles and of the Jews.
consider: Consider that this withdrawal of Jesus signified his passing over to the Gentiles, to whom they were driving him out.
Mystically: before he preached publicly he came into Galilee, which is interpreted 'a passing-over.'
So the preacher of the Gospel must first pass over from carnal things to spiritual, from earthly things to the meditation of heavenly things.
He began where John had left off, preaching the same things John had said before:
that the preaching of the disciple and the Master might be in harmony, and that the Lord might approve and confirm John's teaching.
Rightly, John being delivered up, the Lord's preaching begins; for the Law ceasing, the grace of the Gospel rises, as the sun follows the dawn.
The Law promised earthly and passing goods; but the Gospel promised the heavenly and the earthly — life eternal.
Augustine: John was sent ahead, as the voice before the Word, the dawn before the sun, the herald before the Judge, the servant before the Lord, the friend before the Bridegroom.
And because the darkness and night of unbelief had pressed upon the whole world, and they could not look upon the sun of justice,
blessed John was sent ahead like a lamp — that eyes blinded by the bleariness of iniquity, unable to see the great and true light,
might grow used to the admitted light of the lamp, seeing first as it were a thin splendor;
so that little by little, the cloud of sins receding and the humor of unbelief drained away, at Christ's coming they might be gladdened by that heavenly light rather than driven back.
He said not merely 'say' penance — against those who speak and do not;
nor merely 'purpose' it in the mind — against those who delay;
do not omit it — against the negligent; do not destroy it — against those who fall back again;
but DO penance: as a medicine to heal the disease, as armor to enter the war, as a key to unlock heaven.
For the kingdom of heaven is at hand: as it is set far off by sin, so it is drawn near by penance.
Jerome: Do penance, you who wish to cling to the eternal good, the kingdom of God.
He who desires the kernel breaks the nut; the sweetness of the fruit makes up for the bitterness of the root;
the hope of gain delights amid the perils of the sea; the hope of health softens the pain of the medicine.
prayer: who, having compassion on our misery, and as a true physician willing to give us the medicine of souls,
began your preaching from penance, and bade us, while we have time, to do it:
grant to me, a wretched sinner, the space and the effect of repenting —
you who have so long, in mercy, spared me the offender, for which be praise to you;
that through penance the kingdom of heaven may draw near and be unlocked to me,
which is set far off and shut by sin —
through true repentance and the compunction of tears.
Grant that I may be, in the moment of death, such as you made me to be in the water of regeneration, in the laver of Baptism,
that I may deserve to be fit for the kingdom of heaven. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Praemissus est Joannes, velut vox ante Verbum, aurora ante solem, praeco ante Judicem, servus ante Dominum, amicus ante Sponsum.
latinJewel.english: John was sent ahead, as the voice before the Word, the dawn before the sun, the herald before the Judge, the servant before the Lord, the friend before the Bridegroom.
